item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like how there are so many ways this can be interpreted, and conflicting resolutions to each one. The vague sentence "However, this only takes effect when the interacting human has no prior knowledge regarding this anomalous property" (what is "this"?) adds to the confusion.

  1. There is a memetic hazard causing people to see pigs as people. Maybe created by an anomaly-possessing animal rights group? We should treat this as any other memetic hazard.
  2. There is a reality-bending hazard that causes pigs to become conscious. We should contain the spread with high priority, and contain all the affected pigs and make sure their human rights are respected.
  3. There is a memetic hazard that causes people to perceive others as pigs. Perhaps released by cannibals or meat manufacturers. It doesn't seem to be done that well, since the Foundation can tell they're still people in pig form. We should contain the memetic threat, and in the mean time spread propaganda to ensure pigs are treated humanely (wait.. is that why so many religions ban eating pork?)
  4. There is a reality-bending hazard that causes people to turn into pigs, in body if not in mind. We should contain the spread with high priority, and contain all the affected pigs and make sure their human rights are respected.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is neat :D

though I just want to maybe clarify that the Foundation researchers only think the instances manifested, but in reality, they were just the D-Class being affected by the anomaly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Notes: Three days later, ██ SCP-4381 instances were reported to have manifested in the D-Class Wing.

"Hey, you came back alive! What was it today?"

"I have no fucking clue, man. They told me to go stand in this room, and there's this guy there…"

(+1)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Subject emits human screams. Removed tail resembles the gluteal sulcus muscle found in humans. Further analysis indicates physiological similarities with tails of non-anomalous pigs.

+++1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah. Nagiros asked the same thing during the drafting phase. It's like this SCP became a crossbreed of SCP-4670 and SCP-1152.

I've removed the collapsible already, but it do suppose to add more detail about the actual skip since pretty much everything before the Director's note is obsolete and the entire documentation only serving as an effect of the true anomaly.

Any recommendations on how can I expand the interview log maybe?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Actually, almost everything before the Site-235 Director's note is questionable.

But in short, this is just some sort of memetic or info hazard that makes people perceive other people as pigs. Since it only obscures their perception, other vectors like sound, images and extracted tissue breaks the effect. However, as soon as they think of the SCP, the effect regains momentum.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"However, this only takes effect when the interacting human has no prior knowledge regarding this anomalous property."

So to be clear, they act like humans until you learn that there's an anomaly and then they resume acting like pigs?

I think the collapsible should be scrapped. It's a dud.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It’s pretty similar to SCP-1152, but there’s just enough grisly detail to distinguish this one. Two things stood out to me — the revision in the collapsible contributes nothing, and the log with the D-Class is too short to effectively characterize them or make me sympathize with them.
Very nice, a simple idea that was fun to read, enjoyed it greatly.

item.title: .au
item.content:encoded:
So, I started writing this scip last May 14 this year, but didn't end up makng a drafts thread until just this late September cause I was busy at work and had a ton of personal issues.

▶ See more author notes and possibly spoilers
◀ Close author notes and possibly spoilers

This was originally suppose to be a some sort of soul parasite of the "Oversoul". The Oversoul is like humanity's collective souls and this thing is parasitizing it through humanity. It quickly became a complex idea since I also planning on adding a "pop cap" to humanity due to the Oversoul, where humans being born after 10 billion or 20 billion slowly become deforemd or even anomalous. Anyway, I wasn't really sure how to execute it so I scrapped it immediately.

Months pass, I was doing SEO, and finished my second game, I decided to give this SCP a second try.

This is when I added the "pig" part. It was originally a pig parasite so it just make sense that I add the pigs as a side effect. But then I started getting positive feedback on it. So I realized that yeah, "something memetic obscuring humans and implanting the idea that they're pigs" (as Mr. Crayne put it via PM) was a indeed pretty interesting. Although since this was not suppose to be the idea, I struggled to conceptualize further. I had to consult more people and get their feedback and see which works.

Now, either this SCP is about "The Foundation thinks it's an anomaly that causes humans with knowledge of its properties to perceive certain pigs as having human qualities, while it is in fact an anomaly that causes certain humans to perceive affected humans as pigs" as phrased by Simartar or "you saw and treated them as pigs while you didn't know about their anomalous nature. That their screams sound human if you know what they are, if you hear them talk when you know, but you just hear them squeal and oink when you don't" as suggested by Mr. Crayne.

I pretty much hit bedrock in terms of conceptualizing about this scip so I decided to post it already.

I hope this didn't turn out to be something shit and wasted the effort of people that shaped it.

If you have any ideas or suggestions on how can I further improve this SCP, don't hesitate to comment it down. :)

▶ First round of critique
◀ First round of critique
  • The Pighead
  • Lord_of_Laugh
  • N_Aepic_Fael
  • Nagiros
▶ Second round of critique
◀ Second round of critique
  • DrChandra
  • Gummy_Dragon
  • Simartar
  • cybersqyd
  • Crayne
